title: Ha It is funny because a baby gets dead
display_title: Ha It is funny because a baby gets dead
wikitext:
[]: Ha! It is funny because a baby gets dead.             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Jul 22, 2009 12:58 am The first three times I watched episode 19, I didn't notice that anyone got killed when Unit 02 crashed into the shelter in front of Shinji. I watched it a fourth time a while back and noticed these lines: "Abandon the Sixth Shelter! All survivors, hurry to the Third Shelter!" "Take casualties to the Sixth Block." I suddenly realized something I should have a long time ago: a lot of people were killed offscreen when Unit 02's head crashed into the shelter . The fact that it happened right offscreen and I didn't even notice it was really disturbing to me. Then, today, I watched it a fifth time and noticed you do see some of the people who end up getting crushed: You see them for less than two seconds, but they look happy enough. You can even hear the baby on the right giggling. (Giggling, yes. Much colder than having the baby cry IMO). Several cuts later, I noticed something else I hadn't before: covering the rubble around Unit 02's head is purple blood and... red blood? I turned off the subs and, indeed, you can see two or three corpses. The baby's definitely dead, BTW; he's giggling pretty audibly before the crash and afterwards there's no infant sound to be heard. I'm usually against using the death of a baby you've never seen before as shock value (this was particularly egregious to me in the Battlestar Galactica pilot), but this was done in a masterfully disturbing manner. I didn't even notice until five viewings in. The only thing that does bother me is the ridiculous improbability that Unit 02 lands juuuust in the right place to land right in front of Shinji and not hurt him, requiring an extraordinarily accurate velocity and angle of flight. I made another thread about things like that before, though, so I won't get any further into that. Still, note that Shinji's desire to sit in a corner as far away as possible from other people saved his life. So yeah, SSD said this deserved its own thread. Apparently enough other people didn't notice the carnage to make it worthwhile. For anyone who hasn't caught this yet, you're welcome.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 22, 2009 1:02 am Damn you Anno! ...On the plus side, they could all return after 3I if they wished? But uhh... yeah. That's depressing. EDIT: Shinji looks pretty shocked by all of this by the way, not just the fact it's Eva-02's head in front of him. Warning, Rebuild 2.0  SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Ha! It is funny because a baby gets dead.             Quote Post by Axell » Wed Jul 22, 2009 1:05 am NAveryW wrote: The baby's definitely dead

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 22, 2009 1:07 am Axell wrote: Anno is a cold blooded man

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Wed Jul 22, 2009 1:17 am The gore only serves to show just how determined Shinji is to not pilot Unit 01. Even when Asuka has her Eva's head cut off and people are dying right in front of Shinji, the violence just shows how much pressure has to build up before Shinji will pilot Unit 01. This is probably my favorite episode of the series, for the intense action and the feeling of desperation. On another note, I find it very interesting that Kaji is the one who finally gives the final push to convince Shinji to pilot.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Jul 22, 2009 1:21 am Yeah, episode 19 is now solidified as the most disturbing Evangelion episode ever, even without the Zeruel munching. And that's saying something.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Jul 22, 2009 1:31 am Holy shit. All these years and I never noticed! That's incredible! I had no idea anyone got hurt. Oh man. Now I'm starting to wonder, how many other shots are there like this, where people die as a result of Angel battles? This changes my whole interpretation of that scene -- in particular, Shinji's reaction to seeing Eva-02's head. The first time I saw it, I thought his reaction, including the darkening of the screen, was to reflect his horror that Asuka might have died. Now I'm wondering if his reaction was seeing all those people die and realizing it could have been him. Not that he needs a single specific reason to be freaked out. Come to think of it, it's obviously all of the above: 'Oh my God I almost died', 'Oh my God those people died', and 'Oh my God Asuka might have died'.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Jul 22, 2009 1:42 am Monk Ed wrote: Now I'm wondering if his reaction was seeing all those people die and realizing it could have been him.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Wed Jul 22, 2009 1:48 am or Asuka could just not have sucked. "lets shoot bullets and missiles at it even though it only ever worked once. Yea! What a great and refreshing new plan! I sure hope it doesnt have a shield of some kind, that would suck....."

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Jul 22, 2009 2:02 am Axell wrote: or Asuka could just not have sucked. "lets shoot bullets and missiles at it even though it only ever worked once. Yea! What a great and refreshing new plan! I sure hope it doesnt have a shield of some kind, that would suck....."

[]:             Quote Post by Rock-Slash » Wed Jul 22, 2009 2:05 am I notice it the second time i watched it, it was disturbing but hey, thats war for you, there always are casualties, how many people do you think couldn't make it to the shelters when Unit 00 self-destructed, in ep22 when Asuka shoots that cannon after being hit by Arael those explotions probably killed someone, this case is more disturbing just because we are watching it directly. NAveryW wrote: Not only could it have been him, but he might have been able to prevent the others' deaths if he had chosen to pilot Unit 01 from the start.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Wed Jul 22, 2009 2:47 am Noticed the dead before, but never the baby. Very interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Jul 22, 2009 3:48 am "lets shoot bullets and missiles at it even though it only ever worked once. Yea! What a great and refreshing new plan! I sure hope it doesnt have a shield of some kind, that would suck....."

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Jul 22, 2009 8:55 am I like seeing on screen casualties and thinking of off screen casulties during angel battles. Makes it seem much more epic. Like the thought of somebody might have been crushed during Asuka's and Shinji's synco dance in episode nine.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jul 22, 2009 9:01 am I knew there were casualties (random guy says so) but never noticed the baby or froze the screen there. Nice find!

[]:             Quote Post by BattleMonkey » Wed Jul 22, 2009 9:21 am Axell wrote: or Asuka could just not have sucked. "lets shoot bullets and missiles at it even though it only ever worked once. Yea! What a great and refreshing new plan! I sure hope it doesnt have a shield of some kind, that would suck....."

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Jul 22, 2009 9:38 am Guess that just adds to the whole picture huh. That one guy is really dead haha

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Wed Jul 22, 2009 1:17 pm Considering how much Anno borrowed from Ideon, I would've expected a baby-killing scene to be waaay more explicit.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Wed Jul 22, 2009 1:21 pm ^I guess he figured it would be censored out so he didn't bother besides, it fits together with the patented "GAINAX Budget Shortfall®"

[]:             Quote Post by Logan Payne » Wed Jul 22, 2009 1:39 pm But when you dwell too long on those dead bodies of people we didn't know, it takes a whole different direction.
